attention2.ptxt: Do not use any fluid other than specified ATF. Use of any other fluid may cause juddering or some other faulty condition to occur.

item.ptxt: Be sure to perform ATF level check before checking any service on A/T.
Do not replenish or drain ATF before each test.

servinfosub.title: Level Check at Normal Operating (Hot) Temperature – Hot Check

s1.ptxt: Drive vehicle so that ATF temperature reaches normal operating temperature (70 – 80 °C (158 – 176 °F)).

attention3.ptxt: Engine coolant temperature can be raised by racing engine in “P” or “N” range, but ATF temperature cannot be raised in “P” or “N” range. Be sure to drive vehicle in order to raise ATF temperature.

s1.ptxt: Stop vehicle on a level place with engine running.
With select lever in “P” range, apply parking brake and block wheels.
Connect SUZUKI scan tool to DLC and confirm “ATF Temperature” is 70 – 80 °C (158 – 176 °F).
Keep engine idling, depress brake pedal fully and shift select lever slowly to “L” and back to “P” range.
With engine idling and A/C switch turned off, pull out fluid level gauge (1), wipe it off with clean paper towel and put it back into place.

Pull out fluid level gauge (1) again and check fluid level indicated on it. Lowest fluid level should be between “FULL HOT” (2) and “LOW HOT” (3). If it is below “LOW HOT”, add specified ATF up to “FULL HOT”.

item.ptxt: Do not race engine while checking fluid level, even after engine start.
Do not overfill. Overfilling can cause foaming and loss of fluid through breather. Then slippage and transaxle failure can result.
Bringing level from “LOW HOT” to “FULL HOT” requires 0.4 liters (0.85 / 0.70 US/Imp. pt).
If vehicle was driven under high load such as pulling trailer, fluid level should be checked about half an hour after it is stopped.
Check lower position of fluid on both sides of level gauge.

servinfosub.title: Level Check at Room (Cold) Temperature – Cold Check
servinfosub.ptxt: Fluid level can be checked temporarily at room (cold) temperature which corresponds to 20 – 30 °C (68 – 86 °F). This level check is considered as preparation before performing level check under normal operating (hot) temperature. Checking procedure itself is the same as that described in Level Check at Normal Operating (Hot) Temperature – Hot Check . If fluid level is between “FULL COLD” (4) and “LOW COLD” (5), proceed to test drive. And when ATF temperature has reached 70 – 80 °C (158 – 176 °F), check fluid level again and adjust it if necessary.

attention2.ptxt: Fluid level check at room (cold) temperature is recommended only for preparation of level check under normal (hot) operating condition.
Failure to perform fluid level check under normal (hot) operating temperature may result in damage to transaxle.
